home.hero_title: Find the best game deals in one place
home.hero_subtitle: Game Prices automatically collects prices from leading digital stores, compares them, visualizes price history, and helps you find the best deals without wasting time.
home.feature_price_desc: We provide real-time price comparison from major stores so you always know where the best deal is.
home.feature_history_desc: Track detailed price history to understand trends and choose the perfect moment to buy.
home.feature_wishlist_desc: Save your favorite games to a wishlist and get instant notifications when prices drop.

about.title: About Game Prices
about.subtitle: A temporary educational project built to explore APIs, databases, background syncs, and the small product decisions that make data feel useful.
about.note: This is not a commercial store. It is a learning project focused on building a realistic price-tracking experience while keeping the codebase simple, testable, and honest about what it does.
about.what_is.title: What is GamePrices?
about.what_is.body: GamePrices is a small price-comparison demo for games. It gathers store offers, shows current pricing, and keeps a record of how those prices change over time so the interface feels like a real product rather than a static mockup.
about.why.title: Why does this project exist?
about.why.body: The main goal was learning. GamePrices was created as a temporary non-commercial project to practice working with external APIs, a relational database, scheduled updates, notifications, and the kind of data modeling that turns raw responses into something readable.
about.data.title: Where does the data come from?
about.data.body: Their API helps provide store and price information that can be normalized into a single place, which is ideal for this project because it lets me focus on integration logic instead of inventing fake data.
about.safety.title: Are the shops safe?
about.safety.body: The platform only highlights stores that are considered authorized or otherwise trustworthy sources. The point is not to sell keys directly, but to help users see where a game is offered and what the price looks like before they decide where to buy it.
about.audience.title: Who is GamePrices for?
about.audience.body: It is useful for visitors who just want a quick comparison, for registered users who like saving games and following price drops, and for anyone who wants to study how a small product can be built around live data without turning into a full e-commerce platform.
about.features.title: What do registered users get?
about.features.body: Registered users can bookmark games, keep a wishlist, receive alerts when prices change, and use a more personalized view of the platform. That makes the app feel closer to a real service while still staying inside the scope of a learning project.
about.history.title: Why keep price history?
about.history.body: Current prices are useful, but price history is what makes patterns visible. By keeping past values, the project can show whether a discount is new, repeated, or just part of a normal cycle.
about.sync.title: How often does the data update?
about.sync.body: The sync runs every 12 hours once the app is deployed on the server. Right now, during development, updates are triggered manually.
about.mission.title: What are we trying to learn here?
about.mission.body: The mission is to make the app useful as a study project: learn how APIs are shaped, how data is stored, how relations between games and stores are modeled, and how a simple interface can still communicate something credible.
